sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Bruker (BRKR) Projects Modest Revenue Growth and EPS Increase fo
feed.summary: Bruker (BRKR) has projected modest organic revenue growth and significant improvements in operating margins and EPS for 2026. The company, which operates in the medical devices and instruments industry, anticipates a double-digit boost in non-GAAP earnings per share. Despite a solid 3-year revenue growth rate, Bruker faces challenges in profitability and efficiency, as indicated by a negative net margin and a high RSI suggesting overbought conditions.

feed.title: Manufacturer Saves Untold Hours with Enhanced Time Tracking Solution
feed.summary: A manufacturing and architectural design firm significantly improved its operations by implementing Centrally HR, an enhanced time tracking solution. This led to a 50% reduction in payroll processing time, improved accuracy in timekeeping and scheduling, and increased employee engagement through self-service tools. The integration also eliminated manual time entry and provided real-time labor data for better decision-making.

feed.title: Stock Market Live January 13: S&P 500 (VOO) Rises on Tame Inflation Data
feed.summary: The S&P 500 (VOO) is rising due to tame inflation data, with December's CPI increasing only 2.7% year-over-year, less than expected. This opens the door for the Federal Reserve to potentially lower interest rates twice in 2026. KeyBanc upgrades Intel (INTC) and AMD (AMD) stocks, projecting significant growth for both, especially in their data center and AI segments.

feed.title: Can Trump make single-family homes affordable by banning big investors?
feed.summary: Donald Trump proposes banning Wall Street firms from buying single-family homes to address housing affordability, aligning with some Democrats' criticisms of corporate homebuying. While supporters argue large investors drive up prices and make homeownership elusive, critics contend that banning such investors won't solve the core issue of housing supply shortages and may hinder investment. The proposed ban, intended to be forward-looking, aims to exclude institutional investors while protecting "mom-and-pop" landlords.

feed.title: U.S. Bank to buy BTIG for $1B to bolster capital-markets business
feed.summary: U.S. Bank announced its acquisition of financial services firm BTIG for up to $1 billion, aiming to strengthen its capital markets business. The deal, expected to close by Q2 2026, comprises $725 million in cash and stock, plus $275 million contingent on performance targets. This acquisition builds on an existing referral partnership between the two companies and is intended to fill product gaps for U.S. Bank's clients while providing BTIG clients access to a broader financial platform.

feed.title: Ross Stores Inc stock hits all-time high of 193.19 USD
feed.summary: Ross Stores Inc. (ROST) stock has reached an all-time high of $193.19, surpassing its previous 52-week high, marking a significant increase of 29.32% over the past year and 47.3% in the last six months. This strong performance, despite a challenging economic environment, is supported by a "GREAT" financial health score from InvestingPro and higher-than-expected third-quarter earnings. Analysts have raised price targets for the company, maintaining a Buy rating, while acknowledging potential overvaluation based on its P/E ratio.

feed.title: MGM Stock Downgraded on Lingering Las Vegas Casino Issues
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International's stock has been downgraded to "hold" from "buy" by Truist Securities analyst Barry Jonas due to ongoing Las Vegas Strip challenges, specifically weak leisure demand. Jonas predicts a 9% year-over-year drop in Q4 EBITDAR and a 1% decline for 2026, emphasizing the need for MGM to enhance its customer value proposition, especially at mid-tier properties. The report also touches on potential M&A activities, particularly MGM increasing its stake in BetMGM.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean cancels stops to its Caribbean port through end of 2026
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean has extended its cancellation of visits to Labadee, its private destination in Haiti, through December 2026 due to safety concerns amidst ongoing gang violence and civil unrest in Haiti. This decision follows previous cancellations and means no Royal Caribbean ship will have visited Labadee since April 2025. The cruise line will reroute affected ships to other Caribbean destinations or include sea days, acknowledging the logistical challenges of last-minute port changes and the negative financial impact of these cancellations.

feed.title: Teradyne stock reaches all-time high at 229.87 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Teradyne (TER) stock has reached a new all-time high of $229.87, trading just above its 52-week high, despite InvestingPro's fair value assessment suggesting it's overvalued. The company has seen a 70.92% increase over the past year and a 131.59% gain over six months, driven by strong market confidence, robust financial health, and AI-related demand. Analysts have upgraded the stock, and Teradyne is expanding operations with a new U.S. hub and has declared a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Moody's (MCO): Morgan Stanley Raises Price Target to $526 | MCO Stock News
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained an Equal-Weight rating for Moody's Corporation (MCO) and increased its price target from $520.00 to $526.00 as of January 13, 2026. This adjustment reflects a modest 1.15% increase, while other analysts have recently issued more substantial upgrades. The average target price from 20 analysts for MCO is $551.51, suggesting a 4.12% upside from its current price, with a consensus "Outperform" status.

feed.title: Bank of America stock slips as Trump credit-card cap clouds BAC earnings
feed.summary: Shares of Bank of America (BAC) slipped after President Donald Trump proposed a one-year cap on credit-card interest rates at 10%, adding a new layer of policy risk ahead of the bank's upcoming earnings report. The proposed cap, intended to take effect on January 20, is facing strong pushback from bank trade groups who warn it could lead to account closures and severe restrictions for a significant portion of credit-card holders, particularly impacting subprime borrowers. Investors are closely watching Bank of America's net interest income trends and credit-card performance metrics, as well as any commentary on the potential implications of the rate cap during its Q4 2025 earnings call.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Raises Price Target for JCI, Maintains Overweight Rating | JCI Stock News
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained an "Overweight" rating for Johnson Controls International (JCI) and increased its price target from $125.00 to $130.00. This adjustment reflects continued confidence in JCI's growth potential, with other analysts also revising their targets upwards. Despite an "Outperform" consensus from brokerage firms, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside of 19.01% based on its GF Value calculation.

feed.title: GILD: Morgan Stanley Lowers Price Target for Gilead Sciences | G
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has reiterated its "Overweight" rating for Gilead Sciences (GILD) but revised its price target down to $150 from $151. This minor adjustment comes amidst other analyst revisions, including recent price target increases by UBS and Citigroup, and a prior increase by Morgan Stanley itself. The average analyst price target for Gilead Sciences is $134.45, suggesting a potential upside, while GuruFocus's GF Value estimates a downside.

feed.title: Should You Buy, Hold or Sell Goldman Stock Ahead of Its Q4 Earnings?
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs (GS) is set to release its Q4 2025 earnings, with Zacks analysts expecting a revenue increase but a slight decline in EPS. Key factors influencing performance include solid market-making revenues, strong investment banking fees driven by M&A and IPO activity, and robust net interest income, despite anticipated rises in general expenses and one-time charges from the Apple Card partnership termination. While the company's long-term prospects are strong due to strategic pivots and AI integration, a somewhat expensive valuation and rising costs suggest investors might consider waiting until after earnings for a more appealing entry point.

feed.title: Ameren Corp. Stock: Sleepy Utility Or Low-Key Power Move For Your Portfolio?
feed.summary: Ameren Corp. (AEE) is presented as a stable, dividend-paying utility company that, despite its unexciting nature, could be a valuable addition to a long-term, income-focused investment portfolio. The article highlights its consistent dividends, regulated stability, and gradual investment in cleaner energy, contrasting it with high-growth, volatile stocks. It concludes that Ameren is a "cop" for investors seeking long-term income and stability rather than short-term, high-octane gains.

feed.title: Global Payments completes acquisition of Worldpay
feed.summary: Global Payments has successfully completed its acquisition of Worldpay from FIS and GTCR, simultaneously divesting its Issuer Solutions business to FIS. This strategic move transforms Global Payments into a pure-play commerce solutions provider, significantly expanding its global reach and capabilities to serve over six million merchant locations worldwide. The company aims to leverage enhanced scale and client-centric distribution channels to capitalize on growth opportunities and deliver value to its expanded client base.

feed.title: Why Five9 (FIVN) Stock Is Down Today
feed.summary: Shares of cloud contact center software provider Five9 (FIVN) fell 3.2% after a Barclays analyst lowered the company's price target from $29 to $25, overshadowing news of expanded partnership with Google Cloud. This follows a previous drop two months ago when Five9 issued a disappointing revenue forecast despite solid third-quarter results. The stock is currently trading 55.8% below its 52-week high, raising questions about future growth and market perception.

feed.title: Monthly payouts of $0.0425 set for Franklin Universal Trust in Q1 2026
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton has announced the monthly distributions for certain closed-end funds for January, February, and March 2026. The Franklin Universal Trust (FT) will pay $0.0425 per share each month during this quarter. The announcement specifies record, ex-dividend, and payable dates for these distributions, noting that the press release is for informational purposes and not tax reporting.

feed.title: Decoding Analog Devices's Options Activity: What's the Big Picture?
feed.summary: Whales have shown a noticeably bearish stance on Analog Devices (NASDAQ: ADI), with 55% of detected options trades leaning bearish compared to 33% bullish. Traders are focusing on a price range between $230.0 and $320.0, and while the stock price is up, RSI indicators suggest it may be approaching overbought territory. Analysts have an average price target of $333.0, with ratings ranging from Overweight to Hold.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (ARE)
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is reminding investors of the January 26, 2026, deadline to file a lead plaintiff motion in a class action lawsuit against Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (ARE). The lawsuit alleges that the company made materially false and misleading statements regarding its Long Island City property's value and growth potential, leading to a significant stock drop after its Q3 2025 financial results and a $323.9 million real estate impairment charge. Shareholders who suffered losses are urged to inquire about pursuing claims.

feed.title: Apple Revives Validity Challenge to Wireless-Transmission Patent
feed.summary: The Federal Circuit has revived Apple Inc.'s challenge against a wireless data-transmission patent held by Smart Mobile Technologies LLC. The appeals court ruled that the Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) was wrong to reject an argument raised by Apple concerning the interpretation of claim limitations involving antennae. This decision is part of ongoing litigation where Smart Mobile Technologies has asserted infringement of over a dozen patents against Apple.

feed.title: Power Ford Earns #1 Sales Ranking in New Mexico and Top 10 Phoenix Region Honors for 2025
feed.summary: Power Ford has been named the #1 selling Ford dealership in New Mexico for 2025, also ranking in the Top 10 within the Phoenix Region. The dealership achieved a record-setting 1,698 new vehicle sales in 2025, a 22.6% increase from the previous year, attributing its success to strong team performance and customer satisfaction. This recognition adds to Power Ford's accolades, including being a J.D. Power Dealer of Excellence and "Best New Car Dealership" by Albuquerque The Magazine.

feed.title: Moderna jumps 12% on 2025 projections
feed.summary: Moderna's stock price surged by 12% following the release of its 2025 financial projections. The biotechnology company's outlook for the upcoming fiscal year appears to have positively impacted investor sentiment.

feed.title: Boeing beats Airbus in jet orders for first time since 2018 (BA:NYSE)
feed.summary: Boeing has surpassed Airbus in jet orders for the first time since 2018, reporting 1,175 airplane orders last year compared to Airbus's 1,000 gross sales. This significant achievement for Boeing led to a 2.8% rise in its stock during Tuesday's trading.

feed.title: Ford named America’s 'most iconic' company by Time ahead of GM
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has been named America's most iconic company by Time magazine, topping a list that included General Motors at No. 7. The ranking, which considered commercial success and cultural impact, comes as both Detroit automakers navigate significant shifts in business strategy, especially concerning electric vehicles and policy changes. Time highlighted Ford's historical role in American identity and manufacturing innovation.

feed.title: Utility Blamed For Eaton Fire Only Compensated 82 Victims Two Months Into Program
feed.summary: Southern California Edison's compensation program for the Eaton Fire has compensated only 82 of over 1,800 applicants in two months, sparking criticism from fire survivors and advocates. Concerns include the requirement for participants to waive their right to sue, low payment caps, and unequal compensation for children. The Eaton fire, which burned 14,000 acres and killed 19, is widely attributed to Edison's equipment.

feed.title: DR Horton (DHI) downgrade is due to high rates, says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer attributed the downgrade of DR Horton (DHI) to the impact of persistently high interest rates. He highlighted that despite the company's strong execution, the broader market conditions driven by elevated rates are negatively affecting homebuilders. Cramer suggested that investors should focus on companies with innovative technologies that can thrive even in a challenging rate environment, rather than traditional cyclical stocks.

feed.title: F&G Annuities & Life stock hits 52-week low at 27.69 USD
feed.summary: F&G Annuities & Life Inc.'s stock has reached a new 52-week low of $27.69, marking a significant 32.34% decline over the past year due to investor concerns and market volatility. This downturn comes despite the company reporting strong financial results for the third quarter of 2025, exceeding earnings and revenue expectations.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Issues Positive Forecast for Expedia Group (NASDAQ:EXPE) Stock Price
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley raised its price target for Expedia Group (NASDAQ:EXPE) from $180 to $270, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, though this target implies a potential downside from the current trading price. The online travel company recently surpassed earnings and revenue expectations, and the stock is trading near its 52-week high. Despite the positive outlook from some analysts, the overall consensus is "Hold" with an average price target of $285.16.

feed.title: Docusign adds AI features to simplify contract understanding and preparation
feed.summary: Docusign has introduced new AI-powered eSignature features to simplify contract understanding for signers and streamline document preparation for businesses. These tools provide simplified summaries, allow signers to ask questions, and automate document creation, aiming to reduce confusion and preparation time. Despite its stock trading near a 52-week low, analysts have mixed views on the company's performance and future potential.

feed.title: Huntington Ingalls stock reaches all-time high of 413.55 USD
feed.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc. (HII) stock has reached a new all-time high of $413.55, surpassing its previous 52-week high. The stock has increased by 109.32% over the past year, reflecting strong investor confidence and financial performance, though its RSI suggests it's currently overbought. Recent developments include the successful demonstration of its Sea Launcher system, a $97.7 million contract for the USS Harry S. Truman overhaul, and a contract for new small surface combatant ships.

feed.title: KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Given New $168.00 Price Target at UBS Group
feed.summary: UBS Group has lowered its price target for KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) from $176.00 to $168.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of approximately 30% from its previous close. Despite several other firms also trimming their price targets, the MarketBeat consensus for KKR remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $157.50. KKR recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue, and institutional investors hold a significant majority of the company's shares.

feed.title: Crown Castle terminates infrastructure agreement with Dish, says it's owed $3.5bn
feed.summary: Crown Castle has terminated its wireless infrastructure agreement with Dish Wireless, an EchoStar subsidiary, citing Dish's default on payment obligations. Crown Castle claims Dish still owes over $3.5 billion following the contract termination. This action comes after Dish announced it was discontinuing its network business and selling spectrum to AT&T and SpaceX, leading to disputes over leasing agreements with tower companies.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank upgrades Albemarle stock to Buy on bullish lithium outlook
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has upgraded Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) stock from Hold to Buy and increased its price target to $185 from $125, citing a bullish outlook for the lithium market. The bank raised its 2026 lithium price forecasts by 37% and noted that the valuation reflects other high-growth lithium and battery companies. This upgrade follows similar positive revisions from other analysts like Scotiabank, Baird, and Jefferies, indicating strong market sentiment for Albemarle.

feed.title: $4+ Billion Medical Display Market Outlook, 2035 Featuring
feed.summary: The global medical display market, valued at US$ 2,260.03 million in 2024, is projected to reach US$ 4,218.25 million by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 7.61%. This growth is driven by technological integration in healthcare, an increasing volume of complex medical procedures, and digital transformations in fields like dentistry. LED-backlit LCDs and 2.1 to 4MP resolution displays dominate the market, supported by regulatory compliance and significant investments in digital health infrastructure, particularly in Europe.

feed.title: Boeing (BA) CEO is "Amazing," Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has praised Boeing's CEO, stating he is "amazing" and crediting him for the company's turnaround. Despite past struggles and a stock dip, Cramer noted the company's strong cash flow and increased demand for its aircraft. Bernstein also raised Boeing's share price target, indicating confidence in its improved programs and recent deals.

feed.title: Whitestone REIT (NYSE:WSR) Given Consensus Recommendation of "Buy" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Whitestone REIT (NYSE:WSR) has received a consensus "Buy" rating from five brokerage firms, with an average 12-month price target of $16.00. The company recently announced a $50 million share buyback program and a quarterly dividend of $0.1425, leading to an annualized yield of 4.1%. Whitestone REIT reported Q3 EPS of $0.26, beating revenue expectations, and provided FY2025 EPS guidance, indicating positive outlook for the real estate investment trust specializing in Sun Belt retail properties.

feed.title: JPMorgan says Trump's credit card cap would hurt consumers and the economy
feed.summary: JPMorgan executives, including CEO Jamie Dimon and CFO Jeremy Barnum, are warning that former President Donald Trump's proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates would negatively impact consumers and the economy. They argue it would lead to reduced credit access, particularly for subprime borrowers, and reshape banking operations. The industry was reportedly blindsided by Trump's social media announcement and is pushing back with data suggesting widespread credit restrictions if the cap is implemented, though some analysts dispute these claims.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing
feed.summary: The Zacks Analyst Blog previews the Q4 earnings season kickoff, highlighting major U.S. banks like JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, Goldman Sachs, and Morgan Stanley, as well as AI bellwether Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing. The report also discusses key global economic themes including U.S. CPI data for December, Germany's economic performance, and geopolitical risks stemming from U.S. intervention in Venezuela. Investors are advised to watch these events for insights into market trends and the broader economy.

feed.title: Alphabet Stock Is Still Undervalued According to Analysts - 1 Month GOOGL Puts Yield 2.50%
feed.summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) stock is currently considered undervalued by analysts, with an average price target significantly higher than its current trading price. The article suggests that shorting out-of-the-money put options on GOOGL can provide a substantial 1-month yield, offering a potential annualized return over 25% for investors seeking income and some downside protection. This strategy presents an alternative approach to investing in GOOGL by leveraging high option premiums.

feed.title: This German Grocer Is Opening 180 Stores in the U.S. — Should Walmart Be Worried?
feed.summary: Aldi, the German grocery chain, plans to open over 180 new stores in the U.S. this year, making it the third-largest grocer by store count. This expansion comes as more American shoppers, including affluent households, are opting for discounters over traditional supermarkets. Aldi's growth and strategy focused on efficiency and private-label products pose potential competition for chains like Walmart and Kroger.

feed.title: Applied Materials stock hits all-time high at 309.65 USD
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) stock has reached an all-time high of $309.65, surpassing its previous 52-week high, driven by strong performance and consistent growth in the semiconductor industry. Despite being considered overvalued by InvestingPro data, the company boasts a "GOOD" financial health score, a $245 billion market cap, and has maintained dividend payments for 22 consecutive years. Recent analyst sentiment is positive, with several firms raising their price targets, further solidifying investor confidence in AMAT's future prospects.

feed.title: Pfizer Ends Mid-Stage Lupus Skin Trial, Trimming a Small but Promising Autoimmune Option
feed.summary: Pfizer (PFE) has terminated its Phase 2 clinical trial for PF-06823859, a drug aimed at treating skin symptoms in lupus patients. The termination of this mid-stage study is considered a modest negative for Pfizer's immunology pipeline, removing a potential future asset. While the financial impact is expected to be limited due to the trial's early stage and small size, it may affect investor sentiment regarding Pfizer's long-term growth beyond its core vaccine and oncology businesses.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Mosaic (MOS) Could Change Following Carlsbad Potash Sale To Refocus On Saskatchewan
authors: Simply Wall St Reviewed by Sasha Jovanovic
feed.summary: The Mosaic Company is selling its Carlsbad, New Mexico potash operation for US$30 million to International Minerals Carlsbad, shifting its focus to potash production in Saskatchewan, Canada. This move aims to concentrate capital on higher-return assets, potentially altering Mosaic's long-term operational mix and risk profile. Investors are weighing this strategic refocus against ongoing concerns about commodity cycles, capital intensity, and global potash overcapacity, which could impact the company's margins and growth despite analyst forecasts of increased revenue and earnings.

feed.title: CI GAM to acquire Invesco’s Canadian investment funds
feed.summary: CI Global Asset Management (CI GAM) is set to acquire Invesco Ltd.'s Canadian fund business, encompassing 100 mutual funds and ETFs with $26 billion in assets under management (AUM). This acquisition will boost CI GAM's total AUM to approximately $170 billion and includes a strategic partnership where Invesco affiliates will continue to sub-advise 63 funds. The transaction is expected to close in Q2 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

feed.title: Pfizer stock slips as CEO says obesity cash-pay market is “almost like Viagra”
feed.summary: Pfizer's stock dipped after CEO Albert Bourla compared the cash-pay obesity market to Viagra, acknowledging its larger-than-expected size at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference. The company plans to launch 10 phase 3 trials for its Metsera obesity drugs by the end of 2026, but does not anticipate revenue growth until 2029. Investors are closely watching for Pfizer's upcoming Q4 results on February 3rd for more clarity on its 2026 strategies and obesity pipeline progress, as the company aims to catch up in a competitive market dominated by rivals like Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly.

feed.title: GXO Logistics stock hits 52-week high at 58.0 USD
feed.summary: GXO Logistics stock has reached a new 52-week high of $58.00, reflecting a 34.66% increase over the past year and underscoring investor confidence. The company boasts a market capitalization of $6.53 billion and trades at a high P/E ratio, with InvestingPro data suggesting it's slightly over its Fair Value and in overbought territory based on RSI. Analysts maintain an optimistic "strong buy" consensus with a high price target of $80, indicating further potential upside.

feed.title: Draganfly (DPRO) Shares Soar on Drone Deployment in Europe
feed.summary: Draganfly (DPRO) experienced a 3% stock price increase after its drones were selected for European search-and-rescue operations. Despite financial challenges like negative margins and declining revenue, the company shows strong liquidity and no debt, reflected in a high Altman Z-Score. While valuation metrics suggest potential overvaluation, the drone deployment and favorable policy changes contribute to a positive market sentiment for the technology company.

feed.title: FormFactor stock hits all-time high at 74.88 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: FormFactor Inc (FORM) stock has reached an all-time high of $74.88, reflecting significant growth over the past year and six months. InvestingPro analysis indicates the stock is currently overbought and trading above its Fair Value. The company has also announced strategic developments, including manufacturing consolidation and the acquisition of Keystone Photonics, alongside positive analyst ratings.

feed.title: CRWV INVESTOR ALERT: CoreWeave, Inc. Investors with
feed.summary: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP has announced a class action lawsuit against CoreWeave, Inc. for alleged securities law violations between March 28, 2025, and December 15, 2025. The lawsuit claims CoreWeave overstated its ability to meet customer demand, understated risks from reliance on a single data center supplier, and concealed delays that negatively impacted revenue, leading to significant stock price drops. Investors with substantial losses have until March 13, 2026, to seek appointment as lead plaintiff.
